Hero.subtitle: Developing websites and web applications that solve your business goals

Services.title: Services
Services.desc1: I build everything from landing pages and business websites to complex web platforms and Telegram Mini Apps.
Services.desc2: I take care of the technical routine, so you can focus on growing your product.

Experience.title: Experience
Experience.desc1: My name is Ilya, and I am a web developer. For over 3 years, I've been helping businesses build and launch their projects online:
Experience.desc2: building high-quality applications so your users stay happy, and you have peace of mind regarding the result.

case1.description: Turnkey website and service development. I handled the entire cycle: from the visual interface (buttons, animations) to internal logic (databases, server-side). Helped small businesses and startups launch their products quickly.

case2.description: Creating user-friendly interfaces. This included developing highly popular Telegram Mini Apps and helping startups launch the first versions of their products.

case3.description: Developed the visual part for the MindMatch system. Made the registration and login processes as simple, fast, and secure as possible for users.

case4.description: Accurate conversion of Figma designs into working code. Built responsive websites that look equally great on large monitors and mobile phone screens.

faq1.question: What kind of projects do you work on?
faq1.answer: I work with a wide range of clients: from small businesses needing a high-converting landing page, to startups looking to build a complex platform or a Telegram Mini App. I adapt to your specific needs.

faq2.question: What exactly can you do?
faq2.answer: I build turnkey web projects. This can be a landing page, a corporate website, an online store, a marketplace, or a web application. I can handle just the visual part (what the client sees) or the entire internal logic (payments, databases, user dashboards).

faq3.question: How long does a project take?
faq3.answer: It depends on the scale. A standard landing page or corporate website can be launched in 2–4 weeks. Complex platforms or apps take more time. I can provide exact deadlines after we discuss your task.

faq4.question: How is the cost calculated?
faq4.answer: The price depends on the complexity of the logic, the number of pages, and the features. After our initial chat, I will prepare a free estimate document with a clear breakdown of the project stages.

faq5.question: Do you provide support after launch?
faq5.answer: Yes, absolutely. I don't abandon projects after delivery. You can reach out to me for technical maintenance, adding new sections, or expanding your website's functionality in the future.

Footer.title: Let's Talk
Footer.desc: Need a landing page, a business website, or a complex app? Drop me a message, and we'll discuss the best way to bring your idea to life.

two_pistolets.desc: A GameFi Telegram Mini App in a mafia setting. Players complete daily tasks, build their criminal network (clan), and earn in-game currency to open cases containing real rewards — TON and Telegram Stars.
two_pistolets.concept: The app's visual language is built on noir and mafia aesthetics. The dark color palette contrasts with bright accents like golden stars, chips, and weapons. The naming references cult cinema, setting an exciting tone for the loot box mechanics (Don's Case, Code of Honor).
two_pistolets.scope: Developed the app's structure and interface: case store, daily and sponsored task system (ad viewing, clicks), a multi-level referral system (clans with 10% passive income), and a player profile designed for future gamification (leaderboards, inventory, levels).

code_venture.desc: A platform for buying and selling ready-made SaaS MVPs and production-ready code. The project connects developers, allowing them to safely trade software architecture using an Escrow Protocol and a convenient tech stack search.
code_venture.concept: The project is designed in a modern dark-mode aesthetic with neon blue-purple accents, emphasizing the product's high-tech nature. The interface uses strict typography and elements stylized as a development environment (terminal windows, code snippets) to create a trustworthy and familiar space for the target audience — programmers.
code_venture.scope: Designed and developed a full-fledged web application: landing page, catalog (Discovery Engine) with tech stack and category filtering, a product creation flow with a built-in AI description generator, and a detailed seller profile with dispute statistics and sales analytics.

pashalka.desc: A builder platform allowing businesses to launch their own gamified activities in Telegram. The service includes a fully functional admin panel for app configuration and a client side featuring a mini-game, tasks, and a giveaway system to engage brand audiences.
pashalka.concept: The design system is divided into two environments. The control panel is executed in a strict Dark Mode style with a bright acid-neon gradient in the header, giving the interface a modern, tech-savvy look. The client side (the game itself) offers flexible visual customization — from cartoon viruses to donuts, allowing the visuals to adapt to any brand's style.
pashalka.scope: Developed a comprehensive SaaS solution: created a company dashboard (bot profile setup, game asset selection from a library, tariff/subscription management) and built the B2C Mini App itself (arcade 'item catcher' mechanics, leaderboard integration, task system, and reward store).

upora.desc: A platform for buying and selling digital products from creators worldwide. Users can act as both buyers and creators, listing their work, managing their storefront, and receiving payments through a built-in, reliable acquiring system.
upora.concept: A minimalist light interface supporting a Dark/Light mode toggle. The design is crafted not to distract from what matters most — the visual presentation of digital goods. Clean typography, a spacious card grid, and intuitive navigation create a modern and trustworthy user experience.
upora.scope: Developed the full cycle of the marketplace: a home page with catalog, search, and filtering, detailed product cards with a recommendation block, a user dashboard with role separation (buyer/creator), and seamless checkout integration via Stripe supporting credit cards, Link, Cash App, and Amazon Pay.

moerodnoe.desc: A platform for professional genealogist Dmitry Gribkov and the community of family history keepers. The site presents ancestry restoration services — from archival searches and family tree creation to writing a family book and conducting genealogical expertise.
moerodnoe.concept: The visual concept is built on associations with heritage, nature, and roots: a warm beige-green color palette and background patterns resembling tree branches. One of the main interface features is a floating video widget with the founder's message, accompanying the user as they scroll, building a personal connection and increasing trust.
moerodnoe.scope: Designed and developed an informative landing page. Created service cards (in a slider/carousel format) with detailed pricing descriptions, integrated a gallery of video works and reviews, set up a lead capture form for a free initial consultation, and implemented the logic for a 'sticky' video player.

skin_space.desc: A platform for instant trading, buying, and selling in-game items from CS2 and DOTA 2 for real money. The service offers users safe transactions, low fees, and a guarantee on every trade.
skin_space.concept: The project is executed in a classic gamer aesthetic: a dark background with an accent 'fiery' yellow color echoing the logo. Great attention is paid to high-quality 3D character renders and a clean, uncluttered UI to focus on the inventory items themselves.
skin_space.scope: Developed promotional blocks for the home page, a complex interactive trading interface (inventory panel with search, game and rarity filtering), and a functional user profile linked to Steam data (Trade link, API key, Steam ID64).
